rules.label: OS asset store (AssetsV2)
rules.reason: Directory where the OS stores downloaded assets (wallpapers, models, fonts). System-managed, regenerated on demand — manual cleanup is pointless and risky.

rules.label: Apple Neural Engine cache
rules.reason: Compiled neural-net program for the Neural Engine inside Apple's system caches (Spotlight/duetexpertd). System-owned, OS-managed.

rules.label: Installed MLX package binary
rules.reason: Contents of the installed MLX package (including mlx.metallib). Deleting it breaks MLX and any app that depends on it, and it does not regenerate on its own.

rules.label: macOS system volume (SIP)
rules.reason: The OS itself on a read-only volume protected by SIP. Any change breaks boot/updates. Match as a prefix, excluding the /System/Volumes/Data/ firmlink (which holds normal user data).

rules.label: Apple Neural Engine (ANE) cache
rules.reason: Regenerable cache of ANE-compiled models; can grow to hundreds of GB. Deletion is safe but triggers a one-time recompile (CPU/battery). Show it and let the user choose.

rules.label: Apple system cache
rules.reason: Caches for macOS services (Spotlight, duetexpertd, parsecd, etc.). Managed by the OS and running processes — deletion breaks system behavior. Do not touch.

rules.label: On-device speech ML cache (CoreSpeech)
rules.reason: Compiled speech-recognition/dictation models (CoreSpeech). OS-managed; deletion causes a heavy recompile and degraded dictation.

rules.label: Compiled ANE program
rules.reason: Neural net compiled for the Apple Neural Engine in Apple's system ML caches. Files are protected even from reading; do not touch.

rules.label: OS assets (Siri, voices, ML models)
rules.reason: Assets the OS downloads and removes itself (Siri, voices, dictionaries, models). Managed by mobileassetd; deleting an active voice breaks accessibility.

rules.label: macOS system databases
rules.reason: OS databases: launchd, dyld, configs, licenses, TCC permissions. Deletion breaks services and privacy settings.

rules.label: OS asset store
rules.reason: Backup safeguard for AssetsV2 with a trailing slash.

rules.label: Apple system components
rules.reason: Apple system libraries and agents outside the user's ~/Library. Part of the macOS install, protected by the OS.

rules.label: Codex session history
rules.reason: Full agent session transcripts (rollout-*.jsonl). Personal data plus resume state — irreversible. Do not touch.

rules.label: Codex command history
rules.reason: Log of your Codex inputs (personal data, history, and resume). Auto-deletion is not allowed.

rules.label: Claude runtime versions
rules.reason: Old Claude Code versions clean up themselves, but the active version sits alongside — delete the wrong one and you break the running app. Manual only.

rules.label: Active Claude runtime
rules.reason: Binary and data of the running Claude Code (ClaudeCode.app, active version). Deleting it breaks the running tool.

rules.label: Codex log
rules.reason: Codex log (~136 MB). Deleting it loses your work history. User's discretion.

rules.label: Codex session archive
rules.reason: Archive of past sessions (rollout-*.jsonl). Conversation history is personal. Manual only.

rules.label: Installed Python package
rules.reason: Installed libraries (.py, .so, .dylib, .metallib). Deleting files breaks imports and dependencies. Clean up only via pip uninstall.

rules.label: .app bundle internals
rules.reason: Guts of an installed app (binary, resources, frameworks). Deleting any file breaks the signature and launch. Only the whole .app can be removed.

rules.label: Framework
rules.reason: Contents of a .framework — a shared library that apps and the OS depend on. Deletion breaks everything linked against it.

rules.label: Compiled Metal shaders
rules.reason: Compiled Metal GPU shaders inside a package/bundle. Deleting them crashes the software that loads them (e.g. MLX) at launch.

rules.label: Installed Homebrew package
rules.reason: The Homebrew Cellar holds installed formulae. Deleting files breaks the package and dependencies. Clean up via brew uninstall / brew cleanup.

rules.label: Codex app data
rules.reason: Configs, auth.json, sqlite databases, memory. Lots of personal and work data. Not safe by default.

rules.label: Codex CLI runtimes
rules.reason: Codex runtime binaries (~1.3 GB). Re-downloadable but heavy. If you use Codex actively, you'll have to wait for the download. When in doubt, leave them.

rules.label: npm cache
rules.reason: Cache of downloaded npm packages. Doesn't touch project node_modules; npm restores it on install. Pure junk (offline — you'll have to re-download).

rules.label: pip download cache
rules.reason: Cache of downloaded and locally built wheel packages. Doesn't affect installed packages; pip re-downloads (and rebuilds if needed) on the next install.

rules.label: System/user libraries
rules.reason: Shared libraries that installed software links against. Could be junk from removed packages, or an active dependency. Don't delete without checking.

rules.label: Hugging Face model cache
rules.reason: Downloaded HF models/datasets (often tens of GB). Re-downloadable only with internet; in offline/gated cases recovery is impossible. Show it and let the user choose.

rules.label: PyTorch Hub cache
rules.reason: Downloaded PyTorch Hub weights/checkpoints. Re-downloaded on demand. Files can be large — re-downloading costs bandwidth.

rules.label: Xcode DerivedData
rules.reason: Cache of Xcode intermediate builds and indexes. Recreated on the next build (locked while Xcode is running). Downside: the first build is slower and autocomplete drops out temporarily.

rules.label: SwiftPM source checkouts
rules.reason: Not build artifacts but git sources of dependencies inside .build. Re-fetched on resolve (needs internet and versions from Package.resolved). Separately — user's discretion.

rules.label: SwiftPM (Xcode) build output
rules.reason: Non-standard SwiftPM build folder. Regenerated by rebuilding, but the name is custom and matched by substring — better to show it and let the user choose (offline/edited dependencies).

rules.label: SwiftPM build output
rules.reason: SwiftPM build folder (objects, .pack, indexes). Recreated via swift build / Xcode. Downside: the first rebuild is slower and checkouts are re-fetched.

rules.label: CMake/Ninja build output
rules.reason: Intermediate files from the CMake build generator (.o, dependencies). Recreated on cmake/ninja. Downside: the next build is from scratch.

rules.label: Rust/Java build output
rules.reason: Build output folder (Rust cargo, Maven/sbt). Narrow path /target/debug/ so arbitrary target folders aren't hit. Downside: a full rebuild is slower.

rules.label: Gradle cache
rules.reason: Gradle build cache (Java/Android/Kotlin). Restored on the next build. Downside: dependencies are re-downloaded and the first build is slower.

rules.label: Python bytecode cache
rules.reason: Compiled Python bytecode (.pyc) in the __pycache__ directory. Recreated automatically on the next run. Completely safe to delete.

rules.label: npm dependencies
rules.reason: npm dependencies. Restored via npm install only with a package.json+lockfile and a network; linked/patched/private packages may not come back. Show it and let the user choose.

rules.label: Debug symbols
rules.reason: Symbol files for decoding crash logs. Safe to delete inside .build/DerivedData, but next to a release/archive they're needed to analyze crashes. Without build context — user's discretion.

rules.label: Third-party app cache
rules.reason: Cache of installed non-Apple apps. Usually regenerated, but may contain active session/work data. Safer as user's discretion, not safe.

rules.label: Installer image
rules.reason: An .iso install image. Junk only if the app is already installed — check manually, do not auto-delete.

rules.label: Installer image
rules.reason: An install image. Junk only if the app is already installed — check manually, do not auto-delete.

rules.label: Installer image
rules.reason: An installer disk image. Safe to delete if the app is already installed. Check yourself — not safe blindly.

rules.label: Installer package
rules.reason: An install package. Usually unneeded after installing, but decide manually — not safe automatically.

rules.label: User download
rules.reason: Everything in Downloads is something you downloaded yourself. May be needed. Never safe automatically.

rules.label: App version folders
rules.reason: Old versions can be cleaned, but one of them is active. Auto-deletion is risky — user's discretion.

rules.label: Trash
rules.reason: Things you already threw out. Emptying makes sense, but it's your call — the cleaner shouldn't empty it itself.

rules.label: Log file
rules.reason: Logs are usually regenerated, but may contain history/context. Decide manually, not safe blindly.

rules.label: macOS wallpapers/screen savers
rules.reason: Video wallpapers (aerials) — system-managed, re-download themselves.

rules.label: AI model
rules.reason: Neural-net weights — NOT junk. Large; re-downloadable, but delete only if you're sure the app doesn't need the model.

rules.label: AI model (GGUF)
rules.reason: Model weights in GGUF format. Not junk; delete deliberately.

rules.label: AI model (checkpoint)
rules.reason: Model checkpoint. Not junk; delete deliberately.
